comment: The app was superb until the recent update where they completely changed the UI, which now sucks. The most inconvenient part? I've to tap in the typing space to be able to see a small little history symbol on the top left. And what happens when I click on it? They have categorised my history into languages, like wow. And I don't need the typing space to cover my whole screen. Who's the product/UX guy who thought this kind of revamp was a good idea? Keep these in beta and AB tests, not the main v
key sentence: UI update, history symbol, categorised history, typing space covering screen
the function of activity: The activity facilitates translation and navigation:Search, menu, navigate, history, handwriting, options, text entry, paste, language swap, and settings management for user interaction and feedback.
the sub-task list of state: [{'name': 'Navigate up', 'description': 'Go up one level or return to previous screen'}, {'name': 'History', 'description': 'View history of previous actions or translations'}, {'name': 'Handwriting', 'description': 'Access handwriting input for text entry'}, {'name': 'Enter text', 'description': 'Input text using the on-screen keyboard'}, {'name': 'Paste', 'description': 'Paste copied text into the current input field'}, {'name': 'Swap languages', 'description': 'Exchange the source and target languages'}, {'name': 'Select language (A)', 'description': 'Choose the source language for translation'}, {'name': 'Select language (B)', 'description': 'Choose the target language for translation'}]
